gate_arm0a_env_fidelity.significance: This A100 environment is score-faithful on BOTH clips, including wolverine where the local Mac CPU diverged (-0.0876 IDF1, 2 sw, 9 spectFP). GPU-class environments (H100 detbench, A100 here) agree exactly; the Mac CPU path is the outlier.

pooldiag_phase1.executed: Mac-side artifact-only forensics (~14 min, bar 30) + on-VM end-to-end confirmation
pooldiag_phase1.finding: The frozen production pools were generated by benchmark_person_trackers.py DEFAULTS: detector conf floor 0.18, imgsz 960 - NOT the orchestrator operating point (0.05/1536) the detbench spec asserted. Artifact proof: production pool conf_min = 0.1800/0.1846 exactly; box-level IoU diff shows the extra feeder boxes are ~99% non-GT (spectators); the cov4 gain at 0.05/1536 comes from halved track fragmentation (68->52 ids on burlington), not new GT recall (only ~11 GT-matched extra boxes).
pooldiag_phase1.end_to_end_confirmation: yolo_mech018_960 arm (fresh YOLO26m dets at conf .18/imgsz 960 -> same per-frame BOTSORT feeder -> frozen association+scorer) reproduces the frozen pins EXACTLY: burlington 0.883078/0.711667, wolverine 0.851596/0.760000, all-zero FP axes, delta 0.000000 on every metric. FEEDER_DRIFT was 100% operating-point, 0% construction-path.

variant_p_definition: RF-DETR-L pinned arm-1 raw detections (checkpoint sha256 0f4e20e1..., person class id 1, native 704) filtered to conf >= 0.18 (the confirmed production pool floor) -> per-frame BOTSORT (botsort_no_reid_loose.yaml, the confirmed production-equivalent construction) -> frozen association (margin 1.0 + OSNet, defaults) -> frozen scorer. P inputs md5: burl 24b835a68f76f981ed822f89558b9a42, wolv ea144397231f5ac31fd00e834e04e32d (dets 37175->11697 / 5259->2204 after filter).

owner_hypothesis_test.hypothesis: production pool construction + court-margin kills the wolverine spectator FPs while burlington cov4 stays ~0.99
owner_hypothesis_test.result: HALF-CONFIRMED. Burlington: YES - cov4 0.9933 (~0.99) with all-zero FP axes and IDF1 0.9220 (best row ever recorded on this clip). Wolverine: NO - the production conf floor cuts spectator FPs 16 -> 4 and keeps the 1 switch, but does NOT zero them; IDF1 0.8036 and cov4 0.7233 both remain below the frozen baseline (0.8516/0.76).
owner_hypothesis_test.frozen_card_axes: Under the frozen card's hard axes (ANY new switch or spectator FP = reject), RF-DETR-L variant P still FAILS on wolverine. The burlington gain is large and clean; the wolverine deficit is real and survives production-equivalent pool construction.

runtime: RF-DETR-L detector: 31.2/31.8 ms/f batch-1 (unchanged - reused pinned arm-1 dets, no re-inference). yolo mech-confirm feeder on A100: 44.5/58.2 ms/f (A100 slower than H100's 34.6/38.1 at 1536; recorded for completeness, not a comparison row).

honest_issues: RF-DETR-L detections were NOT re-run on this VM: variant P was built from the detbench arm-1 raw dumps (pinned checkpoint sha256, two-sided md5, person id 1). This is MORE reproducible than fresh inference but means P's detector stage ran on the H100 detbench session; RF-DETR inference is deterministic-in-practice across GPU classes but this was not re-verified.

The detbench spec's premise that the frozen pools used 'the production operating point (conf=0.05, imgsz=1536, per orchestrator.py:617-629)' was WRONG - the pools were built by benchmark_person_trackers.py at conf=0.18/imgsz=960. The detbench FEEDER_DRIFT finding is fully explained by this and the detbench arm 0b protocol note should be read accordingly.

The 'production-equivalent' P rule maps only the conf floor to RF-DETR-L (fixed 704 native input); the imgsz dimension of the operating point has no RF-DETR analog, so P is production-equivalent in pool construction, not in detector input resolution.
Wolverine spectator FPs (4) under P are labeled by the frozen scorer's gate-v2.1 decomposition; no visual audit was performed this lane.
Attempt-2 (H100 ase1-b) briefly showed STAGING before GCE returned stockout and auto-cleaned it; verified absent before the ladder proceeded - no double-VM risk materialized.
